content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Found one correctness gap: CLI terminal run/resume synchronization bypasses the new reconciliation behavior. I also ran `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub --lib`, `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check`, and `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked --features test-stub -- -D warnings`; all passed.

## Amendments

1. The direct CLI terminal-sync path still bypasses the new terminal classification reconciliation. Failed runs call only `reconcile_failure_sync` and return at `src/cli/run.rs:1421`/`src/cli/run.rs:1442`, while completed runs directly update milestone/controller state at `src/cli/run.rs:1467` and `src/cli/run.rs:1503` without calling `reconcile_terminal_review_classifications` or performing the required `br update ... closed`. Because `run` and `resume` call this path before returning to the operator (`src/cli/run.rs:3746`, `src/cli/run.rs:4003`), operator-driven bead-backed runs will skip covered-by-existing comments and proposed-beads records, and success will not close the bead through the new reconciliation path.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The bead requires reconciliation after terminal success or failure before control returns to the operator; this only covers daemon reconciliation and leaves the CLI terminal path inconsistent.
